title: bump: Bumping unzipper to mitigate license issue
author: dubzzz
body:
<!-- Thanks for submitting a pull request! Please provide enough information so that others can review your pull request. The two fields below are mandatory. -->

## Summary

Unzipper patched a license issue in the 0.11.x version (related issue: https://github.com/ZJONSSON/node-unzipper/issues/293). The full diff of this new minor is avilable at: https://app.renovatebot.com/package-diff?name=unzipper&from=0.10.14&to=0.11.2.

With that PR, I only make sure that exceljs will pull or at least allow users to pull the patched version of unzipper.

Fixes https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/2743

<!-- Explain the **motivation** for making this change. What existing problem does the pull request solve? -->

## Test plan

Not applicable

<!-- Demonstrate the code is solid. Example: The exact commands you ran and their output, screenshots / videos if the pull request changes UI. -->

## Related to source code (for typings update)

Not applicable

<!-- List with permalink into source code to prove that changes are true -->

comments.author: ZJONSSON
comments.body: I realized from the diffs that the `.tap` files were not being `.npmignored`.  Just pushed out a patch [0.11.3](https://www.npmjs.com/package/unzipper?activeTab=code) please let me know if there is anything you need.   Will be spending some time over next few weekends to get the library into better shape and moving away from fstreams.

comments.author: ZJONSSON
comments.body: Also I bumped tap to a version that doesn't support node 10.  If exceljs needs to support node 10 I will downgrade tap back to the maximum version that works for v10

comments.author: ZJONSSON
comments.body: I downgraded tap on unzipper master to ensure that the tests match the exceljs test suite. No version bump required as this is only a downgrade of dev dependency

comments.author: k0s15
comments.body: cc @Siemienik, you seem to be the only active maintainer, thanks

comments.author: jjshinobi
comments.body: Can someone please merge this PR? Thanks!

comments.author: wilomgfx
comments.body: Anything blocking this from being merged?

comments.author: juwalter
comments.body: @Siemienik - also would like to ask if you could merge this one? many thanks in advance!!

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
Hi everyone.
I released this patch as prerelease version: 4.4.1-prerelease.0  
If no complains with this version, I'm going to release it as 4.4.1 soon

comments.author: jjshinobi
comments.body:
Can we please also bump archiver to 7.0.1 to fix this Snyk vulnurabiltiy:
```
 ✗ Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime [Medium Severity,https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-INFLIGHT-6095116] in inflight@1.0.6
    introduced by exceljs@4.4.1-prerelease.0 > archiver@5.3.2 > archiver-utils@2.1.0 > glob@7.2.3 > inflight@1.0.6
  No upgrade or patch available
```
It relates to the same issue with license. Also, it allows to remove inflight@1.0.6 from the transitive path which has a vulnerability as well.

comments.author: joserodolfofreitas
comments.body:
Hey guys, can we unblock this? 
There are tons of people waiting for this, and it seems the biggest issue is with the CI tests?
Perhaps we at MUI can offer some active assistance to move this forward. Let us know.

comments.author: alumni
comments.body:
Could we bump `unzipper` further to `^0.12.3`? And `archiver` to `^7.0.1`?

`unzipper @ 0.11.6` depends on `fstream` which is deprecated. And both `unzipper @ 0.10 || 0.11` and `archiver @ 5` depend on `inflight` which is deprecated and automatically triggers security warnings because it causes memory leaks, even if `exceljs` doesn't end up using them 🙄

comments.author: SekSion
comments.body: Is there any update for this?, it is still an issue and it is the only license that is making issues for me

comments.author: alumni
comments.body:
I actually started working on a PR for it, but stopped at some point.

I have a problem with running anything in this repo since it doesn't have reproducible builds (when I run build or test locally they fail because it's not deterministic - probably because there is no lockfile). So I ended up upgrading almost everything (would mean releasing v5), fixing some bugs, but currently I have one test that still fails and I didn't want to invest more time if PRs are not merged.

You might want to try `@jsreport/exceljs` - seems it contains some of the fixes that I also ended up doing. However, contributions to that are also a bit difficult since it just moved the code from this repo, so it still doesn't have reproducible builds.

That, or simply use overrides to force the newest versions and patch `exceljs` to fix the `StreamBuf.pipe` issue.

Either way, we need some good fork (none so far) or someone to pick up work on this repo (would be great if the current maintainers could find someone willing to take over).
